differentials.name: rapidly progressive unilateral proptosis / globe displacement
differentials.raw: rapidly progressive unilateral proptosis / globe displacement

differentials.name: 80-90% 5-year survival after biopsy / surgical debulking followed by radiation therapy
differentials.raw: 80-90% 5-year survival after biopsy / surgical debulking followed by radiation therapy (4,000-5,000 rad) + chemotherapy (vincristine, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin)

differentials.name: Dermoid cyst
differentials.raw: Dermoid cyst (cystic appearance, internal fat attenuation, calcification, fluid level, bone depression near zygomaticofrontal suture)

differentials.name: Capillary hemangioma
differentials.raw: Capillary hemangioma (growth during 1.0-1.5 year of life, ipsilateral cutaneous hemangiomas, peripheral + internal flow voids)

differentials.name: Venous-lymphatic malformation
differentials.raw: Venous-lymphatic malformation (multiloculated cystic with ill-defined border, fluid-fluid levels, peripheral enhancement, phleboliths)

differentials.name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
differentials.raw: Langerhans cell histiocytosis (pronounced bone destruction, diabetes insipidus)

differentials.name: Lymphoma (lacrimal gland involvement, encasement of globe, hypointense on
differentials.raw: Lymphoma (lacrimal gland involvement, encasement of globe, hypointense on

differentials.name: abdominal pain + distension (from bladder outlet obstruction), urinary retention, palpable bladder
differentials.raw: abdominal pain + distension (from bladder outlet obstruction), urinary retention, palpable bladder

differentials.name: urinary frequency + dysuria
differentials.raw: urinary frequency + dysuria (← urinary tract infection)

differentials.name: strangury
differentials.raw: strangury (= painful urge to void without success)

context: =  rapidly growing aggressive malignancy frequently invading adjacent bones and soft tissues Origin:   pluripotential mesenchymal cell with capacity to differentiate into skeletal muscle (rhabdomyoblasts resemble primitive striated muscle cells; NOT from striated muscle) Frequency: 4-10% of cancers in children < 15 years of age (ranking 4th after CNS neoplasm, neuroblastoma, Wilms tumor); 5-15% of all adult sarcomas; 35% of pediatric sarcomas ◊  Most common soft-tissue tumor in childhood (60%) Incidence:   4.5 (1.3)÷1,000,000 annually in USA in white (black) Age:   bimodal age distribution: 1-
